regime_fall.current_status: Acute multi-front crisis with US/Israeli military pressure intensifying, Iran reimposing Hormuz blockade despite ceasefire expiration, and internal power struggle between diplomatic and hardline factions paralyzing decision-making. Tehran's negotiating team fractured with leadership changes amid economic hemorrhaging and unresolved succession dynamics.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 24, 2026, Iran reimposed the Strait of Hormuz blockade with IRGC vessels firing on commercial shipping, while Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stepped down as head of the negotiating team amid internal disputes over diplomatic strategy versus military escalation, with Abbas Araghchi positioning to control negotiations.
